statement: This record remains negative evidence on build 26100: the repo did not produce enough supporting proof to promote it into a normal actionable surface.
gating_reason: This record is cross-layer verified and also aligned with a shipped one-click surface.

links.title: Microsoft Learn: Defender CSP HideExclusionsFromLocalAdmins
links.summary: Microsoft documents HideExclusionsFromLocalAdmins with 0 or not configured meaning exclusions stay visible to local admins and 1 meaning local admins can no longer view exclusions.

links.title: Microsoft Learn: Configure exclusions in Defender Antivirus
links.summary: Microsoft notes that HideExclusionsFromLocalAdmins affects whether local admins can view exclusions and that the setting does not remove exclusions from policy storage.

links.title: Windows Defender dump list includes root and Policy Manager HideExclusionsFromLocalAdmins
links.summary: The local Defender dump list shows HideExclusionsFromLocalAdmins under both HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows Defender and HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows Defender\Policy Manager.

links.title: Win25H2Clean baseline visibility with managed exclusion present
links.summary: With both HideExclusionsFromLocalAdmins surfaces unset, a managed exclusion under the policy exclusions branch still appeared in Get-MpPreference and remained present in the managed policy hive.

links.title: Win25H2Clean root-path read for HideExclusionsFromLocalAdmins = 1
links.summary: After writing HideExclusionsFromLocalAdmins = 1 on the root Defender policy path, wmiprvse.exe and powershell.exe both queried HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows Defender\HideExclusionsFromLocalAdmins and read Data: 1.

links.title: Win25H2Clean visibility change with root-path HideExclusionsFromLocalAdmins = 1
links.summary: With the root policy path set to 1, the managed exclusion still existed on the policy exclusions branch, but Get-MpPreference returned 'N/A: Administrators are not allowed to view exclusions'.
